senses.definition: an amount that is very small in comparison to what is desired or needed to have a significant effect

examples.x: Your donation of $10 to the charity is appreciated, but it's just a drop in the bucket comThe repair costs for the old building are astronomical, and our budget allocation is a mere drop in the bucket.
In the grand scheme of things, a single missed opportunity is just a drop in the bucket of your life's journey.
The company's profit margin is so vast that a minor loss in one department is considered a drop in the bucket.

senses.definition: jobs or things that are different in type and small either in size, number, or significance

examples.x: The puzzle was missing a few bits and pieces, making it impossible to complete.
She found bits and pieces of seashells and driftwood along the beach.
His notes were in disarray, with bits and pieces of information scattered across his desk.
The attic was filled with bits and pieces of old furniture, toys, and memorabilia.
The chef used bits and pieces of different ingredients to create a unique and flavorful dish.

word_list.pronunciation: ɪn dɹˈɪbz ɔːɹ dɹˈɪps ænd dɹˈæbz

senses.definition: used to describe something happening or being available in very small amounts

examples.x: She saved money in dribs and drabs, so it took a while to reach her savings goal.
Progress on the project has been slow, with work happening in dribs and drabs.
Donations for the charity arrived in dribs and drabs, making it difficult to plan the program.
The rain came down in drips and drabs, with brief showers followed by clear skies.
He shared details of the story in drips and drabs, making it difficult to understand the entire narrative.

senses.definition: a score that is equivalent to zero in a match or game

examples.x: The team scored a goose egg in the first inning of the baseball game.
She received a goose egg on her quiz because she didn't answer any of the questions correctly.
After searching the house thoroughly, they found a goose egg—no hidden treasures at all.
The company's profit margin for the quarter was a disappointing goose egg.
He attempted to win the raffle, but he ended up with a goose egg—no prizes at all.

senses.definition: having finished one's supply of something very recently

examples.x: They checked their inventory, but apparently they're fresh out of the blender that's on sale.
I wanted to make pancakes for breakfast, but we're fresh out of eggs.
I was planning to watch a movie tonight, but it seems we're fresh out of popcorn.

senses.definition: to be careful to consume or use something in moderation

examples.x: Go easy on the salt when seasoning the soup; you can always add more later.
She needs to go easy on the sweets if she wants to maintain a healthy diet.
During the workout, the trainer reminded everyone to go easy on their joints to prevent injuries.
Go easy with the spices in this dish; you don't want it to be too spicy.
When giving feedback, it's important to go easy with your criticism and offer constructive suggestions.

senses.definition: the small or insignificant details  of something

examples.x: The contract was reviewed with great care, and every jot and tittle was examined to ensure accuracy.
The chef followed the recipe to the letter, making sure not to miss a single jot or tittle in the preparation.
When designing the user interface, the team paid attention to every jot and tittle to create a seamless experience.
The legal team combed through the document, ensuring that no jot or tittle of the regulations was overlooked.

word_list.pronunciation: mˌeɪk pˌʊt ɐ dˈɛnt ɪn ˌɛstˌiːˈeɪtʃ

senses.definition: to lessen the amount of a thing, particularly tasks or money

examples.x: The fundraising campaign made a dent in our financial goal, but we still need more contributions.
She's been working diligently on her project and has already put a dent in her to-do list.
Regular exercise and a healthy diet can put a dent in your weight loss journey.

senses.definition: a person or thing that is really challenging or almost impossible to find

examples.x: Finding that antique book in the used bookstore was like looking for a needle in a haystack.
Locating a specific document in that cluttered office is like finding a needle in a haystack.
Identifying the cause of the computer error in the complex code was like searching for a needle in a haystack.
Trying to find a parking space during rush hour in the city is akin to finding a needle in a haystack.
Spotting a rare bird species in the dense forest is like searching for a needle in a haystack.

senses.definition: various small things that have no importance or value

examples.x: We packed just about everything, but probably left some odds and ends behind.
I found a drawer full of odds and ends—paperclips, buttons, and old keys.
After moving, they had a box of odds and ends that didn't fit into any category.
The garage was filled with tools, paints, and various odds and ends.
She likes to collect odds and ends from her travels as souvenirs.

word_list.word: the smell of an oily rag

senses.definition: the smallest amount of something, particularly fuel or money, that is necessary for surviving or functioning

examples.x: This trusty little car may not look like much, but she runs on the smell of an oily rag.
During the early days of their business, they operated on the smell of an oily rag, cutting costs wherever possible.
She managed to renovate her apartment on the smell of an oily rag by doing most of the work herself.
The theater company survived on the smell of an oily rag, relying on passionate volunteers and donations.
In times of economic downturn, many families have to live on the smell of an oily rag, making tough financial choices.

senses.definition: existing or available in very little quantities

examples.x: Job opportunities in this field are thin on the ground these days.
During the drought, water sources became thin on the ground in the region.
Support for the unpopular policy was thin on the ground among the members of parliament.
In the small town, entertainment options are thin on the ground, so people often travel to nearby cities.
At the end of the season, fresh produce can be thin on the ground at the local market.

senses.definition: to have a limited amount or number of something left

examples.x: We're down to our last few dollars in the budget for this month.
After hours of driving, we're down to a quarter tank of gas; we need to find a gas station soon.
I'm down to my last few pages in this notebook; I need to buy a new one.

senses.definition: due to the absence or lack of a specific thing

examples.x: The project failed for the want of adequate funding.
For want of a key, the door remained locked, and she couldn't enter her own house.
For want of communication, misunderstandings arose, and their friendship was strained.
For the want of proper planning, the project faced delays, and the budget exceeded its limits.
For want of a timely decision, opportunities were missed, and the business suffered.

senses.definition: to experience a sudden setback, failure, or decline

examples.x: After a strong start, the company took a tumble in the stock market due to unexpected losses.
She was at the top of her class, but her grades took a tumble when she started missing classes.
The team had a promising season until their star player took a tumble and suffered a season-ending injury.
The politician's popularity took a tumble after a series of scandals were revealed.
The project was going well until a critical mistake caused it to take a tumble.

senses.definition: a distance or amount that is very small or inconsiderable

examples.x: He missed the target by a hair's breadth, almost hitting the bullseye.
The two candidates' positions on the issue are separated by a hair's breadth; there's hardly any difference.
She escaped the accident by a hair's breadth, narrowly avoiding a collision.
The pricing of the two products is identical, with only a hair's breadth of a price difference.
The competitors in the race finished with a hair's breadth between them, making it a thrilling finish.

senses.definition: not easy to find or restricted in quantity

examples.x: Passionate love like you find in a poem has always seemed scarce as hens' teeth in my experience.
Genuine antique coins from that era are as scarce as hens' teeth.
In this desert region, rainfall is scarce as hens' teeth.
Finding a parking space in this busy city can be as scarce as hens' teeth.

senses.definition: in limited or small quantities, typically referring to something that should be experienced or consumed in moderation

examples.x: I enjoy watching horror movies, but only in small doses because they can be too intense for me.
We tried the spicy dish, but in small doses, as we didn't want it to be overwhelmingly hot.
She will have to approach her new medication in small doses to see how her body responds.
They are slowly introducing him to public speaking, in small doses, to build his confidence.
The children were watching TV in small doses to ensure they didn't spend too much time in front of the screen.
He will be practicing his guitar skills in small doses to avoid straining his fingers.
